supabase_docs_basis: Supabase CLI: supabase test db runs pgTAP tests on local or linked projects
Supabase docs: RLS tests should authenticate seeded personas and assert positive and negative visibility/write cases

proof_boundary: This is a static acceptance-test plan and pgTAP template. It does not apply RLS migrations, start Supabase, connect to hosted Supabase, or prove current deployed policy behavior.

runnable_after: owner approves table classifications and public exceptions
current hosted/local policy state is inspected
narrow identity_and_review RLS migration is drafted
test seed helpers are created or Supabase tests helpers are installed
supabase test db runs locally and, after approval, against linked project

personas.purpose: Prove anonymous users cannot read or write private identity/review rows.
Prove the row owner can read and write only their own permitted rows.
Prove a different authenticated user cannot read or mutate private rows.
Prove reviewer access is limited to review-queue or explicitly assigned review rows.
Prove admin/reviewer override paths are explicit and role-bound.
Prove trusted edge functions and jobs can seed/manage rows without granting public policies.

open_schema_gaps.gap: no durable owner column in current migration; add analysis_run_id/user_id or restrict to admin/service-role only

tables.owner_model_to_confirm: user_id, created_by, reviewer assignment, or service role
tables.minimum_seed: owner private run, unrelated private run, review-queue run, completed public-exception candidate
tables.direct_public_exception: only if an owner-approved published/redacted status is introduced; completed alone is not enough for enterprise-private rows

test_cases.assertion: results_eq count from analysis_runs visible to anon is 0
results_eq count from analysis_runs for unrelated authenticated user is 0
results_eq count from analysis_runs for owner is 1

test_cases.expected: reviewer sees only approved review-queue or assigned rows
test_cases.assertion: results_eq reviewer-visible rows from analysis_runs match approved review seed only

test_cases.expected: service role can perform trusted setup/edge-function operation
test_cases.assertion: lives_ok service-role operation for analysis_runs
throws_ok anon insert into analysis_runs with SQLSTATE 42501

test_cases.expected: write rejected unless row owner or approved reviewer/service role
test_cases.assertion: throws_ok unrelated authenticated write to analysis_runs with SQLSTATE 42501

tables.owner_model_to_confirm: user_id, created_by, reviewer assignment, or service role
tables.owner_path: review owner through analysis_runs.user_id, plus reviewer_id when the actor is the assigned reviewer
tables.reviewer_path: get_user_role() in ('reviewer', 'admin') and reviewer_id::uuid = auth.uid() for writes
tables.minimum_seed: review linked to owner run, review linked to unrelated run, assigned reviewer, unassigned reviewer

test_cases.assertion: results_eq count from human_reviews visible to anon is 0
results_eq count from human_reviews for unrelated authenticated user is 0
results_eq count from human_reviews for owner is 1

test_cases.expected: reviewer sees only approved review-queue or assigned rows
test_cases.assertion: results_eq reviewer-visible rows from human_reviews match approved review seed only

test_cases.expected: service role can perform trusted setup/edge-function operation
test_cases.assertion: lives_ok service-role operation for human_reviews

tables.reviewer_path: security-definer role lookup only; direct reviewer reads need owner approval and column minimization
tables.minimum_seed: owner user row, unrelated user row, reviewer user row, admin user row

test_cases.assertion: results_eq count from users visible to anon is 0

results_eq count from users for owner is 1

test_cases.expected: reviewer sees only approved review-queue or assigned rows

tables.minimum_seed: asset linked to owner run, asset linked to unrelated run, asset linked to review-queue run
